title: Promises for Tomorrow
learningGoal: The learner will compose sentences predicting future events using 'Will', strictly demonstrating the omission of morphological suffixes (no '-ing', '-ed', or third-person '-s') on the trailing verb.
instructions: Think about what you will do tomorrow. Write 3 short sentences promising what you will do, what a friend will do, and what you both are 'going to' do together. REMEMBER THE CLEAN VERB RULE: Do not put 'ing', 'ed', or 's' after 'Will'!

contentItems.text: CRITICAL RULE: DO NOT WRITE 'I will going'. That is totally broken English! Write 'I will go'.

writingPrompt: Write 3 sentences about future plans. Sentence 1 must use 'I will...'. Sentence 2 must use 'He/She will...'. Sentence 3 must use 'We are going to...'.
promptGoal: Test strict adherence to the bare infinitive requirement following modal 'will' and periphrastic 'going to'.
referenceHints: Sentence 3 (Together): We are going to [clean verb]...

modelExampleOutputs.targetText: I will wake up early. She will work hard. We are going to eat pizza.
modelExampleOutputs.transliteration: आई विल वेक अप अर्ली। शी विल वर्क हार्ड। वी आर गोइंग टू ईट पिज़्ज़ा।
modelExampleOutputs.baseTranslation: मैं जल्दी उठूंगा। वह कड़ी मेहनत करेगी। हम पिज़्ज़ा खाने वाले हैं।
modelExampleOutputs.annotation: Perfect score. The learner used 'will' correctly without suffixing the verbs (no 'wakes', no 'works'). Used 'going to eat' flawlessly.

modelExampleOutputs.targetText: I will going to shop. He will plays football. We are going eat.
modelExampleOutputs.transliteration: आई विल गोइंग टू शॉप। ही विल प्लेज़ फुटबॉल। वी आर गोइंग ईट।
modelExampleOutputs.annotation: Critical failure. Overstacked 'will' with 'going'. Violated the clean verb rule by writing 'plays'. Missed the 'to' in the last sentence. Needs heavy review.

evaluationCriteria.description: PENALIZE HEAVILY if the user attaches ANY suffix to the verb following 'will' (e.g., will going, will played, will eats).
Did the user correctly form 'We are going to [verb]' without duplicating 'will'?
Were there 3 sentences fulfilling the prompt parameters?

adminCorrectAnswerSet.sampleAnswer: I will work tomorrow. He will watch a movie. We are going to walk in the park.
acceptableAlternates: I will eat breakfast. She will sleep. We are going to play football.
adminCorrectAnswerSet.grammarBaseline: Absolute zero tolerance for suffixation on verbs following modal 'will'.

feedbackRules.onPass: Magnificent! You wrote perfect future sentences! You proved you understand the Clean Verb rule because you didn't add any extra letters after 'will'.
feedbackRules.onFail: Please review what you wrote. If you wrote 'I will playing', it is completely broken! WILL acts as a soap that washes the verb. It must be 'I WILL PLAY'!
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good start! Don't let the word 'He' trick you. Yes, usually we say 'He plays'. But if 'Will' is there, the 's' is destroyed! It must be 'He will play'.

audioAssets.audioTranscript: Write three promises for tomorrow. I will. He will. We are going to.

hintText: Keep it fast and simple. [Person] + [Will] + [Clean Action]. 'We will run'.
